result.summary: automl.googleapis.com exposes a Google Cloud AutoML HTTP API at https://automl.googleapis.com, authenticated with Google Cloud OAuth access tokens and, where supported by Google Cloud APIs, API keys.
result.description: Google Cloud AutoML is Google's managed service for training and using custom machine learning models with limited ML expertise. The `automl.googleapis.com` domain is the service endpoint for that AutoML API within Google Cloud.

gcp_oauth_access_token.label: Google Cloud access token
gcp_oauth_access_token.setup: Create or select a Google Cloud project in the [Google Cloud Console](https://console.cloud.google.com/), enable the AutoML API in the API Library, then authenticate using Google Cloud credentials. For local use, the easiest path is to install the [gcloud CLI](https://docs.cloud.google.com/sdk/docs/install-sdk) and run `gcloud auth application-default login` for Application Default Credentials or `gcloud auth login` / `gcloud auth print-access-token` for REST calls. Service accounts can also be used via [Application Default Credentials](https://docs.cloud.google.com/docs/authentication/provide-credentials-adc).

gcp_api_key.label: Google Cloud API key
gcp_api_key.setup: In the [Google Cloud Console Credentials page](https://console.cloud.google.com/apis/credentials), create an API key under your Google Cloud project. Restrict the key to the needed APIs and origins as described in [Manage API keys](https://docs.cloud.google.com/docs/authentication/api-keys). Note that Google Cloud documents API keys as usable only for APIs that accept them; many resource-based Cloud APIs instead require OAuth access tokens.

variables.name: project
variables.description: Google Cloud project ID in resource paths.

variables.name: location
variables.description: Regional location in resource paths such as `projects/{project}/locations/{location}`.
